prompt: Develop a NutriDeclare Offline Compliance System for workplace wellness programs to capture employee health profiles, manage phased nutrition goals, and control the full lifecycle of related declarations and deliverables in a secure, internet-free environment. The English-language Vue.js web interface supports three roles: Participants (employees), Reviewers (dietitians or wellness coordinators), and Administrators (program operators). Participants can register and sign in locally, complete a guided health profile, and maintain goal planning with phased milestones (for example, a 12-week plan broken into 3 phases) while viewing prior versions with clear “what changed” summaries and timestamps. When a participant submits a declaration package for review, the UI presents status states such as draft, submitted, withdrawn, corrected, and voided, with contextual actions gated by role and current state. Reviewers work from a queue that highlights upcoming deadlines (e.g., “review due by 04/15/2026 5:00 PM”) and can request corrections with structured issue feedback; participants acknowledge and resubmit, preserving version history. For delivery & asset distribution, the interface provides a delivery list per case (final plan PDFs, supporting raw exports, and revision notes), shows delivery status, enables bulk package download, and requires client acceptance confirmation; downloads can be restricted by permission and set to expire (default 72 hours) with clear on-screen messaging when a link is no longer valid. In-app notifications alert users to status changes, mentions in feedback, review requests, and approaching deadlines, and each user can mute non-critical notifications while keeping mandatory compliance alerts enabled. The backend uses FastAPI to expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js frontend over the local network or on a single machine, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for accounts, profiles, declarations, versions, delivery records, and audit trails. Authentication is strictly offline with username and password only; passwords must be at least 12 characters, include three of four character classes, and cannot match any of the last 5 passwords, with account lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Sessions use signed JWT access tokens (30-minute lifetime) with refresh tokens (7 days) stored hashed in PostgreSQL; optional CAPTCHA is generated and validated locally to reduce automated guessing. RBAC is enforced for both menus and APIs, and administrators can disable/deactivate accounts and issue offline password resets that require a new password at next login. Declaration lifecycle rules are validated server-side, preventing illegal transitions (for example, a voided record cannot be corrected) and writing an immutable audit log for every create/submit/withdraw/void/correction action, retained for 7 years with configurable archiving. Import/export supports CSV and spreadsheet formats with admin-defined field mapping; exports can be masked by policy (for example, showing only allergy categories while hiding free-text medical notes) and all exported files include a local checksum for tamper detection. Scheduled jobs run inside the FastAPI process to rebuild search indexes nightly, refresh reviewer workload stats hourly, and clean up expired download tokens and old notification records, keeping the system performant without any third-party services, email, SMS, or external APIs. Sensitive fields are encrypted at rest using database-side encryption, and all file assets are stored on local disk with database pointers and permission checks on every download.
